channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: AI Consulting: Own Your Own Domain
executive_summary: Nate B Jones emphasizes that successful AI consultants excel by combining deep domain expertise with specific AI knowledge, rather than relying on vague buzzwords. He highlights the importance of established client relationships for scaling quickly and warns against 'AI-washing,' which can damage credibility. The approach involves focusing on a niche, using living templates for customization, and building a trusted network for comprehensive service delivery.
bullets: Consultants who already own client relationships can scale fastest, as noted by Nate B Jones. This means that if you have existing clients, you can leverage those relationships to introduce AI solutions more effectively. Think of it like a gardener who already has a thriving garden; they can easily add new plants without starting from scratch.

Buzzword-heavy pitches like 'AI-powered everything' destroy credibility, according to Jones. Clients are looking for clear, technically sound answers, such as when to use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). It's like trying to sell a car by just saying it's 'fast' without explaining how it performs on the road; potential buyers want specifics.

Owning a specific domain allows you to wrap AI around deep sector expertise, similar to a strangler fig that grows around a tree. This strategy enhances your authority and pricing power in that niche. If you’re a healthcare consultant, for example, your AI solutions will be more valuable because you understand the industry intricacies.

AI allows templates to evolve in real time, making services sharply scoped yet highly customizable for each engagement. This means that instead of static documents, you can create living templates that adapt to client needs. Think of it like a chef who adjusts a recipe based on the ingredients available, ensuring every dish is tailored to the occasion.

Building a trusted network is essential because no one consultant covers the full value chain. Cultivating specialist partners ensures you can bring the right expertise to every project. It’s like assembling a sports team where each player has a unique skill set; together, they can win the game.

Publishing detailed, NDA-safe insights publicly helps separate you from generic 'AI consultants' and fuels referrals. By sharing your knowledge, you establish credibility and attract potential clients. Imagine a chef who shares their secret recipes; it builds trust and draws in diners eager to taste their creations.

Jones states, 'We don’t need more AI slogans; we need consultants who can answer, ‘When should I use RAG—and why?’ This highlights the need for consultants to provide actionable insights rather than flashy jargon. It’s like a doctor who explains the exact treatment plan instead of just saying, 'You need medicine.'

A strangler-fig strategy works effectively, as mentioned by Jones. By growing your AI expertise around the trunk of the domain you already dominate, you can enhance your consulting offerings. Think of it like a tree that grows stronger by wrapping around a sturdy support; it becomes more resilient over time.

If you engage in 'AI-washing' today, you risk losing deals and poisoning the ecosystem tomorrow, according to Jones. This means that overhyping your AI capabilities can lead to distrust among clients and peers. It’s like a fisherman who exaggerates the size of their catch; eventually, no one will believe them.

Jones emphasizes the need for specificity in AI consulting. By articulating concrete deliverables, you can better meet client expectations and justify higher fees. It’s like a contractor who provides a detailed blueprint; clients feel more secure knowing exactly what to expect.

The importance of a narrow niche cannot be overstated. By focusing on a specific area, you can develop deeper expertise and command higher fees. Think of it like a specialist doctor who charges more than a general practitioner because they offer targeted knowledge and skills.

Jones suggests that consultants should leverage existing books of business or build one through clear, public content. This approach showcases your expertise and helps attract new clients. It’s like a writer who builds an audience by consistently sharing valuable stories; over time, they gain loyal readers.
key_takeaways: Focus on building and leveraging existing client relationships to scale your consulting business. This matters because established trust can accelerate your ability to introduce AI solutions. If you don’t, you may struggle to gain traction in a competitive market.

Avoid using buzzwords in your pitches; instead, provide clear, actionable insights. This is crucial because vague claims can lead to lost credibility and clients. If you fail to do this, you risk being seen as just another generic consultant.

Commit to specializing in a specific domain to enhance your authority and pricing power. This is important because deep expertise allows you to offer tailored solutions that clients value. If you skip this step, you may find it hard to differentiate yourself from competitors.

Publish detailed insights regularly to establish credibility and attract referrals. This is vital because sharing knowledge can set you apart from the sea of generic consultants. If you don’t, you may miss opportunities to connect with potential clients.

By the end of the year, aim to develop living templates that can adapt to client needs in real time. This is essential because customizable solutions can increase client satisfaction and retention. If you don’t prioritize this, your services may become outdated and less appealing.

technical_terms.AI-washing: The practice of using buzzwords and vague claims about AI capabilities without providing substantive details. Think of it like a product that claims to be 'organic' but doesn’t actually meet any real standards.
technical_terms.RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation is a method that combines information retrieval with AI generation to provide more accurate and contextually relevant answers. Think of it like a librarian who not only finds books but also summarizes their contents for you.
technical_terms.Living templates: Templates that can evolve and adapt in real time based on client needs and engagement specifics. Think of it like a smartphone app that updates automatically to improve functionality and user experience.
technical_terms.Domain expertise: Deep knowledge and understanding of a specific industry or field. Think of it like a seasoned chef who knows the ins and outs of Italian cuisine, making them more skilled than a general cook.
technical_terms.Value chain: The full range of activities that businesses engage in to deliver a product or service. Think of it like a relay race, where each runner has a specific role to play in getting the baton to the finish line.
technical_terms.Niche: A specialized segment of the market for a particular kind of product or service. Think of it like a small bookstore that focuses exclusively on rare books, attracting a dedicated clientele.
technical_terms.Consulting: The practice of providing expert advice to organizations in a particular field. Think of it like a coach who helps athletes improve their performance through tailored training.
technical_terms.Partnerships: Collaborative relationships between consultants and specialists to enhance service offerings. Think of it like a band where each musician plays a different instrument, creating a harmonious sound together.
technical_terms.Content marketing: The strategy of creating and sharing valuable content to attract and engage an audience. Think of it like a farmer who shares tips on growing crops to build a community of loyal customers.
technical_terms.Credibility: The quality of being trusted and believed in based on expertise and experience. Think of it like a seasoned pilot whose track record gives passengers confidence in their safety.
technical_terms.Customization: The process of tailoring a product or service to meet specific client needs. Think of it like a tailor who adjusts a suit to fit perfectly, ensuring the client looks and feels their best.
technical_terms.Referrals: Recommendations from satisfied clients that lead to new business opportunities. Think of it like word-of-mouth advertising, where happy customers spread the news about a great restaurant.
